connections.gloss: abide, accomplish, [idiom] be clearer, confirm, continue, decree, [idiom] be dim, endure, [idiom] enemy, enjoin, get up, make good, help, hold, (help to) lift up (again), make, [idiom] but newly, ordain, perform, pitch, raise (up), rear (up), remain, (a-) rise (up) (again, against), rouse up, set (up), (e-) stablish, (make to) stand (up), stir up, strengthen, succeed, (as-, make) sure(-ly), (be) up(-hold, -rising).
(arise, be up, get (oneself) up, rise up) early (betimes), morning.
arise, lift up, raise up (again), rise (again), stand up(-right)
connections.def: from 303 and 2476; to stand up (literal or figurative, transitive or intransitive):--arise, lift up, raise up (again), rise (again), stand up(-right). see GREEK for 303 see GREEK for

connections.gloss: awake, lift (up), raise (again, up), rear up, (a-)rise (again, up), stand, take up
connections.def: probably akin to the base of 58 (through the idea of collecting one's faculties); to waken (transitively or intransitively), i.e. rouse (literally, from sleep, from sitting or lying, from disease,…

connections.gloss: arise, rise (up), as soon as it is up.
appoint, establish, make, raise up self, (a-) rise (up), (make to) stand, set (up).
connections.def: {to rise (in various applications, literal, figurative, intensive and causative)}
